reviews.title: Dead by Daylight
reviews.excerpt: The open world FROM always needed to build. Every corner hides something worth finding, and the lore rewards obsession. I have 300 hours and I still find new things.

reviews.title: Blue Protocol
reviews.excerpt: Gorgeous art style, combat that clicks once you stop fighting the camera. Character creator alone is worth the download.

reviews.title: Hollow Knight
reviews.excerpt: A masterclass in atmosphere. Hallownest is one of the most fully realized worlds in gaming. The final boss nearly broke me.

reviews.title: Dungeon Meshi
reviews.excerpt: Uses food as a lens for grief, survival, and what it means to be human. Studio Trigger is doing Miura's source material full justice.

reviews.title: Frieren: Beyond Journey's End
reviews.excerpt: A meditation on time, memory, and what we leave behind. Quietly the best anime of the decade so far. Every episode hit differently.

reviews.title: Cyberpunk: Edgerunners
reviews.excerpt: 10 episodes. Did more with the Night City setting than 100 hours of the game. David Martinez lives rent-free in my head.

reviews.title: Berserk
reviews.excerpt: Thirty years in. Still the most ambitious dark fantasy ever committed to paper. Miura's legacy continues with Studio Gaga.

reviews.title: Vinland Saga
reviews.excerpt: Started as a revenge story and became something far more profound. The Farm Arc is peak manga. Thorfinn's character arc is flawless.

reviews.title: Chainsaw Man
reviews.excerpt: Fujimoto does not care about your expectations. Part 2 went in a direction no one predicted and I respect it deeply.

reviews.title: Your Name
reviews.excerpt: Rewatched for the third time. Still cried. The comet scene does something to me every single time without fail.

reviews.title: Spirited Away
reviews.excerpt: The gold standard. Every frame is a painting. Every character unforgettable. Miyazaki at his absolute peak.

reviews.title: The Boy and the Heron
reviews.excerpt: Dense and personal in a way that demands multiple watches. Miyazaki making something for himself and trusting the audience to keep up.
